Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a single exam question, including its statement, options, correct answer, and detailed explanations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Question entity.

statement.description: The main text or enunciado of the exam question.

optionA.description: The text content for option A of the question.

optionB.description: The text content for option B of the question.

optionC.description: The text content for option C of the question.

optionD.description: The text content for option D of the question.

correctOption.description: The letter ('A', 'B', 'C', or 'D') indicating the correct option for the question.

justification.description: A detailed explanation justifying why the correct answer is indeed correct.

distractorExplanation.description: An explanation of why the incorrect options (distractors) are wrong, or common pitfalls associated with them.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category entity this question belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Question)

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification or topic for questions (e.g., 'Redes', 'Bases de Datos').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the question category.

description.description: An optional brief description of the category.

UserExamSession.title: UserExamSession
UserExamSession.description: Records a single instance of a user taking an exam, tracking its progress and final outcome.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserExamSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the external user's unique identifier. (Relationship: User 1:N UserExamSession)

startTime.description: The date and time when this exam session began.

endTime.description: The date and time when this exam session concluded.

score.description: The total score achieved by the user in this exam session.

percentage.description: The percentage of correct answers in this exam session (0-100).

correctAnswersCount.description: The total number of questions answered correctly in this session.

incorrectAnswersCount.description: The total number of questions answered incorrectly in this session.

totalAttemptsUsed.description: The cumulative sum of all attempts made across all questions in this session.

efficiencyScore.description: A calculated metric indicating the user's efficiency, typically the average number of attempts per question.

userQuestionAttemptIds.description: References to individual UserQuestionAttempt entities that comprise this exam session. (Relationship: UserExamSession 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

UserQuestionAttempt.title: UserQuestionAttempt
UserQuestionAttempt.description: Tracks a user's interaction and outcome for a single question within a specific exam session, including multiple attempts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserQuestionAttempt entity.

userExamSessionId.description: Reference to the UserExamSession this attempt belongs to. (Relationship: UserExamSession 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

questionId.description: Reference to the Question entity that was attempted. (Relationship: Question 1:N UserQuestionAttempt)

attemptsMade.description: The number of attempts the user made for this specific question (maximum 3).

isCorrect.description: Indicates if the user eventually answered this question correctly within the allowed attempts.

selectedOptionHistory.description: An ordered list of options ('A', 'B', 'C', or 'D') selected by the user for this question across all their attempts.

finalSelectedOption.description: The option ('A', 'B', 'C', or 'D') the user selected on their last attempt for this question, if any.

definition.description: Top-level collection for question categories. These are public-readable to allow users to browse categories, with write access restricted to administrators. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is global/admin-controlled.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for a question category.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all exam questions. These are public-readable for all users taking exams, with write access restricted to administrators. No denormalized authorization fields are required as access is global/admin-controlled.

Subcollection under a specific user, storing all exam sessions for that user. Access is restricted to the owner (`userId` in path). The `userId` in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context for independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (from request.auth.uid).

params.name: examSessionId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific user's exam session.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual question attempts within a specific user's exam session. Access is restricted to the owner of the parent session (`userId` in path). The `userId` from the parent path provides the necessary denormalized authorization context.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user (from request.auth.uid).

params.name: examSessionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent user's exam session.

params.name: attemptId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific user's attempt at a question within an exam session.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Path-Based Ownership to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **Public Data (Categories, Questions):** For `/categories/{categoryId}` and `/questions/{questionId}`, these collections are designed for public read access (e.g., all authenticated users can read). Write access would be restricted to administrators, managed via an independent roles collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`), thus avoiding any `get()` operations for authorization checks.
2.  **User-Owned Data (UserExamSession, UserQuestionAttempt):** Authorization for `/users/{userId}/examSessions/{examSessionId}` and `/users/{userId}/examSessions/{examSessionId}/questionAttempts/{attemptId}` is achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into the path. This denormalizes the authorization context. Firebase Security Rules can then directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents or external collections to verify ownership. This ensures atomic operations and simplifies rule logic considerably.

**QAPs (Queries are not Filters):**
1.  **Global Data (`categories`, `questions`):** Since these collections are intended for all authenticated users to read, `list` operations are inherently secure. Rules can simply allow `read` if `request.auth.uid != null`, ensuring that queries return all visible data without needing server-side filtering.
2.  **User-Specific Data (`/users/{userId}/examSessions`, `/users/{userId}/examSessions/{examSessionId}/questionAttempts`):** `list` operations on these collections are naturally secure due to the path-based ownership. A user can only query their own path (i.e., where `{userId}` matches `request.auth.uid`). Firestore's security rules will enforce this path-level authorization, guaranteeing that `list` queries only return data belonging to the requesting user, thereby satisfying the QAPs principle without complex rule logic.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (e.g., `categories`, `questions`, `examSessions`, `questionAttempts`) maintains a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents within `/questions` have the same access requirements (public read), and all documents within `/users/{userId}/examSessions` are privately owned by that specific user. This prevents mixing data with different access needs in a single collection, simplifying security rules and making them easier to reason about.
